summary: The provided text contains only the metadata, title, and reference list for a 2015 article by Terrie E. Moffitt and Amber Beckley. Because the full text is unavailable, extraction is strictly limited to the core argument presented in the title: a warning against prematurely discarding twin studies in favor of epigenetics in criminology.

principles.statement: Do not prematurely abandon twin research in favor of epigenetic research.
principles.rationale: While epigenetics is an emerging area of interest, advising criminologists to completely replace established twin research with epigenetic research is premature.
principles.source_quote: ABANDON TWIN RESEARCH? EMBRACE EPIGENETIC RESEARCH? PREMATURE ADVICE FOR CRIMINOLOGISTS

signature_quotes.text: ABANDON TWIN RESEARCH? EMBRACE EPIGENETIC RESEARCH? PREMATURE ADVICE FOR CRIMINOLOGISTS
signature_quotes.context: The title of her 2015 response regarding the state of biosocial criminology.

anti_patterns.description: Advising criminologists to abandon twin research to exclusively embrace epigenetic research.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It is 'premature advice,' indicating that epigenetic research is not yet in a position to completely replace the methodological value and insights of twin studies.
